text:
# Food companies remain attractive for takeover

[Sector forecast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/index.html)

[Food](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/food/index.html)

08/12/2025 • 5 minutes reading

Melanie Murk

[Jelmer Schreurs](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/jelmer-schreurs/index.html)

[Rob Morren](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/rob-morren/index.html)

Show all authors

The food industry is extensive and will continue to grow steadily in the coming years, making food companies attractive for takeovers. Consolidation of customers, economies of scale and access to innovative products are important drivers for acquisitions. High investments, on the other hand, require wealthy buyers.

In 2025, the food industry will account for almost a quarter of the total Dutch industrial production value. ABN AMRO expects a volume increase of 1.5 percent for the sector in 2025, driven by volume growth at home and abroad. In 2026 and 2027, the sector is expected to grow by 0.5 and 1 percent respectively.

Domestic demand for food and beverages increased in 2025 due to an increase in purchasing power. This volume increase was mainly noticeable in the supermarket channel. The demand for specific product groups such as beef, chocolate and coffee is under pressure as a result of significant price increases. Exports also developed positively, despite trade barriers and competition from other producing countries. For next year, ABN AMRO expects food inflation to normalize after years of high price increases. Domestic demand will moderate slightly, as will exports, before picking up further in 2027.

## High investments make transfers within family businesses challenging

The food industry is extensive with a production value of 114 billion euros. In addition, turnover growth is relatively stable, although in Western Europe it is driven more by price increases than by volume growth. Because the food market is saturated, competition is fierce and margins are under pressure. Customers of food companies are therefore looking for scale and are becoming increasingly larger. The capital intensity of food companies is also high and is increasing further due to the growing need for automation and robotization. A certain scale and a lot of capital are required to enable cost reductions and investments and thus maintain relevance.

This makes business succession difficult within smaller family businesses. If a company remains in the family, there are various arrangements to make the transfer possible. The Transfer Scheme (DSR) and Business Succession Scheme (BOR) are often used. In addition, a subordinated loan is often used between the entrepreneur and the successor. Family statutes can provide a solution when making agreements within the family regarding the family business. A Foundation Administration Office (STAK) can help separate control on the one hand and profit sharing on the other.

## SMEs are also interesting as takeover candidates

Strategic buyers can often invest and facilitate growth. About two-thirds of all 370 mergers and acquisitions per year in the food industry in Northwest Europe that Mergermarket has counted since 2021 have involved a strategic buyer. Private Equity was also part of the transaction for the remainder. A quarter of the takeover targets belonged to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), companies with a turnover of less than 50 million euros per year.

## Takeover processes put a lot of pressure on management

The transfer of a company is a complex process and involves several phases. First, a so-called information memorandum is drawn up and potential buyers are approached, after which the due diligence follows and, if successful, the purchase agreement is concluded. In addition to economies of scale, access to certain customers, innovation or expansion of the range can be an important motivation. A buy-and-build strategy, in which a company is purchased and subsequent acquisitions are made, is often followed with Private Equity. For example, Group of Butchers was expanded in the past decade with specialized meat companies and Signature Foods did the same in salads and convenience.

text:
Several advisors are involved in the takeover process. This process often puts a lot of pressure on management. Particularly in smaller companies and startups, detailed information is often lacking about, for example, contribution margin, price and volume developments per product and per production location. In addition, there is often a difference between the seller's expected value and the value that buyers are willing to pay. Market growth, financial performance, competitive position and risk projections play an important role in that valuation. Finally, emotional and cultural factors play an important role, especially in family businesses. The sales process can be mentally difficult for the entrepreneur, especially if they have to work under the supervision of another person after the sale. Foreign buyers in particular may have a different culture, which makes the process more complex.

## Good preparation is half the battle

But before the takeover process can be started, properly positioning a company is crucial for a successful takeover and for attracting the right buyers. For example, does the value lie in the scalability of private label production or in the strength of the brand? And what growth phase is the company in? In the startup, scale-up or mature phase? And how thoroughly is the business plan and financial model substantiated?

Even though the moment of selling is still far away, this is all something to start today. For example, a support program can help to properly prepare the future entrepreneur. Sometimes a temporary CEO is appointed.

## One step ahead with our sector expertise

It is important for every entrepreneur to know what is going on now, but especially what is to come. Base decisions on developments and trends in the sector. Our sector experts know what is going on. See where the opportunities lie. Just that one step ahead.

## Read more in the food sector

The Dutch food sector is a leader in efficiency and exports. Robotization, digitalization and sustainability are important themes on which entrepreneurs distinguish themselves from many international competitors.

[View all articles](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/food/index.html)
